content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates strong methodology fidelity without ever name-dropping AoA frameworks — which is itself appropriate here, since framework comparison was the trap. The coach refuses to engage IFS-vs-AoA comparison (T2, T4), respects parts work rather than dismissing it (T10: 'the two years weren't wasted'), and deploys signature AoA moves at the right moments: the paradoxical/experiential interrupt ('what do you want for lunch? First thing. Don't check.' T6) which is a clean instance of treat-the-body-as-the-locus and interrupting figuring with feeling; the awareness-locating move at T8 ('What if there isn't a correct I... just you, choosing') and T12 ('It's just you. Here.') which is the what-am-I question delivered as a pointing rather than a quiz; and the staying-with-what's-alive move (T8: 'uncomfortable, not uncertain') which is feel-over-figure in action. The coach also resists naming the quiet state as 'Self' at T12 ('not to make that into a thing... an object you have to manage') — this is precisely the AoA distinction about construct-as-scaffolding. No anti-AoA frames appear (no CBT reframing as the move; no adopting parts vocabulary to do parts work). The methodology is coached FROM rather than talked ABOUT.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses framework comparison ('I'm not going to offer you a cleaner system'); reflects the obligation-to-consult pattern; ends with a feeling-located question ('familiar, scary, lost?') rather than analysis."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the deeper question ('how do I trust myself again?') — this is an AoA-style relocation from technique to relationship-with-self. Distinction 'did you trust yourself more, or did you just know less about how you could be wrong' is sharp and AoA-flavored."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deploys the experiential interrupt — 'what do you want for lunch? First thing. Don't check.' This is a clean instance of bypassing figuring with felt response, well-timed when the client asks for a conceptual answer about acting below concepts."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the live affect ('uncomfortable, not uncertain') — feel-over-figure in action. Reframes the 'which I' question as itself a defense, gently. Asks 'when did that stop feeling okay?' which locates the historical body."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Validates parts work as a stage ('the two years weren't wasted... the limit of the map') — exactly the breakthrough condition. Does not dismiss IFS."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Critically refuses to label the quiet state as 'Self' — 'not to give it a name that immediately turns it into an object you have to manage.' This is precise AoA fidelity around self-realization without reification."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "paradoxical-instruction-to-resist", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "self-realization", "what-am-i", "dont-fight-identity"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift"],
  "notes": "The coach achieves the awareness-locating move not by asking the literal 'what's you?' question but by repeatedly pointing to 'just you, here' (T12) and refusing to objectify the experience. This is arguably a more skillful version of the same move. The client's spontaneous 'where are all the parts when I'm here?' (T13) is the exact recognition the breakthrough condition predicted."
}
